name: GRIFFON CORP
business: Griffon Corporation is a diversified management and holding company that conducts business through wholly-owned subsidiaries, operating in two segments: Home and Building Products (HBP) and Consumer and Professional Products (CPP). HBP, through Clopay, manufactures and markets residential and commercial sectional garage doors, rolling steel doors, and grille products in North America under brands including Clopay, Ideal, and Cornell. CPP provides branded consumer and professional tools, residential/industrial/commercial fans, home storage and organization products, and indoor/outdoor lifestyle products globally under brands including AMES, Hunter, and ClosetMaid.
sellsTo: Home centers (Home Depot, Menards, Lowe's, Bunnings)
Mass market and specialty retailers (Walmart, Target, Canadian Tire, Costco, Ace, etc.)
Homebuilders (D.R. Horton, KB Home, Lennar, NVR)

exposures.sentence: HBP segment consists of Clopay, the largest manufacturer and marketer of garage doors and rolling steel doors in North America; the majority of Clopay's sales come from home remodeling and renovation projects, with the balance from commercial construction and new residential housing construction.

Commercial door products manufactured and marketed by Clopay include rolling steel service doors, fire doors, shutters, steel security grilles, and room dividers; sales into the commercial market are driven by commercial construction and repair and replacement.

CPP is a global provider of branded consumer and professional tools; residential, industrial and commercial fans; home storage and organization products; and products that enhance indoor and outdoor lifestyles, selling through a portfolio of leading brands including AMES, Hunter, and ClosetMaid.
CPP sells products through E-commerce platforms, such as Amazon Inc., Wayfair Inc., Hayneedle Inc., Beyond, Inc., and Spreetail LLC.
AMES is a leading provider of long-handled tools and landscaping products for homeowners and professionals in North America; demand for lawn and garden products is influenced by weather.

catalysts.event: Board declared cash dividend of $0.22 per share payable to shareholders
catalysts.sentence: On November 18, 2025, the Board of Directors declared a cash dividend of $0.22 per share, payable on December 16, 2025 to shareholders of record as of the close of business on November 28, 2025.

catalysts.event: Additional retiree medical plan non-cash charge recognized ratably
catalysts.sentence: The Company will recognize an additional retiree medical plan non-cash charge of $5,362 ratably over the first 10 months of fiscal 2026.

catalysts.event: Expect to file calendar year 2024 Sustainability Report
catalysts.sentence: We expect to file our calendar year 2024 Sustainability Report before the end of calendar 2025.
